# File Connector - Full Documentation

# Using Anypoint Code Builder to Configure File Connector

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

**Open Beta Release**: The **cloud IDE is in open beta**. Any use of Anypoint Code Builder in its
beta state is subject to the applicable beta services terms and conditions, available from the IDE.

To use Anypoint Code Builder to configure a connector or module, create a basic integration project
in Anypoint Code Builder, add connectors and other components to your Mule application to process
your business logic, and configure the attributes.

## Before You Begin

Before creating an integration project, you must:

- [Set up and access the Anypoint Code Builder web or desktop IDE](../../anypoint-code-builder/start-acb)
- Have credentials to access the connectorâs API
- Ensure that the Visual Studio Code Extension Pack for Java is installed.

To use this connector, you must be familiar with:

- The connectorâs API
- Anypoint Connectors
- Mule runtime engine (Mule)

## Creating a New Integration Project

To create a new integration project:

1.  In the activity bar of the IDE, click the
    ![Anypoint Code Builder icon](../../connectors/_images/acb-icon-ms-logo.png) (**Anypoint Code
    Builder**) icon.
2.  From **Quick Actions**, click **Develop an Integration**:

    ![Develop an Integration link highlighted in the Getting started section](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/develop-integration-option-mat.png)

3.  Complete the following in the **Develop an Integration** form:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Attribute</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Project Name</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unique name for your project.</p></div><div><p>This name is used as the title and name of the integration project file. For example, if the project name is "Salesforce Integration," the project file name is <code>salesforce-integration</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Project Location</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Your home directory or another directory you create. Click <strong>Browse</strong> to select a different directory for the integration project.</p></div><div><p>Donât create the project within another project directory.</p></div><div><p>See <a href="../../anypoint-code-builder/start-add-folders">Adding Folders to Your Home Directory</a>.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

4.  Select the type of project to create by selecting either the **Empty Project** or **Template or
    Example** card.
5.  Select the **Mule runtime** and **Java Version** for your app.
6.  Click **Create Project**.

## Adding the Connector to Your Integration Project

Anypoint Connectors provide operations for retrieving, modifying, and sending data to and from
systems.

In addition to the
[built-in connectors](../../anypoint-code-builder/acb-reference#builtin-connectors) that Anypoint
Code Builder provides, you can download many other connectors from Anypoint Exchange.

To import a connector from Exchange and add it to your configuration:

1.  In the Explorer view, open the configuration XML file for your project, such as
    `my-project-name.xml`.
2.  Click the
    ![Show Mule graphical mode icon](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/icon-tree-view.png) (**Show
    Mule graphical mode**) icon in the activity bar to open the canvas UI if it doesnât open
    automatically.
3.  Add the connector the same way you added other components from the canvas UI:
    1.  In the canvas UI, click the
        ![Add component icon](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/icon-plus.png) (**Add component**)
        icon.
    2.  In the **Add Component** panel, click **Connectors**.
    3.  Click the connector name and then click the operation to add, such as **Publish**:

        ![Add Publish operation from Anypoint MQ Connector](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/int-add-connector-operation.png)

        If the connector is not available locally, click the
        ![Search in Exchange icon](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/icon-search-exchange.png)
        (**Search in Exchange**) toggle:

        ![Search in Exchange toggle](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/int-connector-search-exchange.png)

        <table><tbody><tr><td><i data-value="1"></i><b>1</b></td><td>Search locally</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="2"></i><b>2</b></td><td>Search in Exchange</td></tr></tbody></table>

    4.  Select the connector to add to your project.
    5.  Select the operation from the **Add Component** panel.

Adding a connector to an integration project does not make that connector available to other
projects in your Anypoint Code Builder workspace.

## Configuring the Trigger For the Flow

A trigger (source) initiates a flow when a specified condition is met. You can configure one of
these triggers to use with File Connector:

- **On New or Upated File**

  Triggers a flow for a newly created or modified file

- **HTTP > Listener**

  Initiates a flow each time it receives a request on the configured host and port

- **Scheduler**

  Initiates a flow when a time-based condition is met

  For more information, see
  [Scheduler (<scheduler/>)](../../anypoint-code-builder/acb-component-scheduler).

For example, to configure the **On New or Updated** trigger:

1.  Add the **On New or Updated** operation to the canvas UI.
2.  Click the **On New or Updated** operation on the canvas UI.
3.  In the **General** tab, configure the following attributes:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Attribute</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Directory</p></td><td><p>Directory where polled files are located.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Recursive</p></td><td><p>If selected, uses files contained in sub-directories.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Matcher</p></td><td><p>Matcher used to filter events on files that donât meet the matcherâs criteria.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Watermark mode</p></td><td><div><div><p>If selected, picks only files that were created or updated after the last poll was executed.</p></div><div><p>Options include:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>DISABLED</p></li><li><p>MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP</p></li><li><p>CREATED_TIMESTAMP</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Time between size check</p></td><td><p>Wait time, in milliseconds, between size checks to determine if a file is ready to read. This allows a file write to complete before processing. Mule runtime performs two size checks and waits the specified amount of time between calls. If both checks return the same value, the file is ready to read.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Time between size check unit</p></td><td><p>Time unit for <strong>Time between size check</strong>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Scheduling strategy</p></td><td><p>Triggers the flowâs execution based on a time-based condition. This field is not currently enabled in the configuration UI. Configure it in the XML editor. See <a href="#scheduling-strategy">Configuring the Scheduling Strategy in XML</a>.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="3"><p><strong>Post processing action</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Auto delete</p></td><td><p>If selected, deletes each processed file after itâs processed, which causes all files obtained in the next poll to be treated as new files.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Move to directory</p></td><td><p>Directory path to which to send each processed file after it processes. This is an alternative to <strong>Auto delete</strong> if you donât want to delete the file.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Rename to</p></td><td><p>Renames a file to this value when it is moved. This attribute works in tandem with <strong>Move to directory</strong>. The connector retains the original file name if you donât configure this field.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Apply post action when failed</p></td><td><p>If selected, applies any of the post actions (<strong>Auto delete</strong> and <strong>Move to directory</strong>) in case the file processing fails. If you de-select this option, no failed files are moved or deleted.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Overwrite</p></td><td><p>Overwrites the target file when the destination file has the same name.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Adding Components to Your Project

Add components to your integration project to build a flow:

1.  In the **Explorer** view, open the XML file for your project, such as `my-project-name.xml`.
2.  Select **Build a Flow** from the start card to create an empty flow:

    ![Anypoint Code Builder canvas with starting cards with the options to start from scratch, build a flow, or build a subflow](../../connectors/_images/acb-build-flow.png)

3.  Change the name of the flow:
    1.  Click **Flow**.
    2.  Click the edit icon.

        ![Arrow pointing to the flow name edit pencil icon](../../connectors/_images/acb-change-flow-name.png)

    3.  Enter the flow name, and click the checkmark.
    4.  In the canvas UI, click the
        ![Add component icon](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/icon-plus.png) (**Add component**)
        icon.
    5.  In the **Add Component** panel, search for and select your component from the results. The
        following example shows the **Listener** component from the **HTTP** search results:

        ![Listener component highlighted in the Add Component section](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/main-tutorial-add-first-listener.png)

        The configuration XML file now includes the XML for the HTTP Listener into the `<flow/>`
        element, for example:

        ```XML
        <flow name="getFlights" >
          <http:listener path="" config-ref="" doc:name="Listener" doc:id="ojzfry" />
        </flow>
        ```

## Adding a Global Configuration

Add another component, this time using the XML configuration menu. For example, add the
`<http:listener-config/>` component from a snippet.

For more information about snippets, see
[Configuring Components](../../anypoint-code-builder/int-configure-components).

1.  In the configuration XML, place your cursor before the `<flow/>` element and type `http`.

    Ensure that the cursor is not inside the `<flow/>` element.

2.  Type `http`, and select the `http:listener-config` snippet:

    ![http:listener-config snippet highlighted in the configuration XML menu](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/add-http-config-snippet.png)

    The snippet adds the following code:

```xml
<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" >
  <http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
</http:listener-config>
```

## Configuring Component Attributes

After you add components to your integration project, configure the attributes in the component UI,
or configure the attributes directly in the XML code editor.

Connector XML tags start with a namespace followed by an operation, following the syntax
`namespace:operation`, such as `<db:select/>` for a **Select** operation in Anypoint Connector for
Database (Database Connector).

You can use autocomplete from an expression (**fx**) field for a component to select DataWeave
functions from the Core module and other values accepted in this field.

### Configuring the Copy Operation

The **Copy** operation copies the file or directory specified in **Source Path** into the **Target
Path**. The source path can be either a file or directory. If **Source Path** points to a directory,
then the directory is copied recursively.

1.  Click the **Copy** operation on the canvas UI to configure the following attributes in the
    **General** tab:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Attribute</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Source path</p></td><td><p>Path to the file or directory to perform the operation on.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target path</p></td><td><p>Path to the directory to place the file in.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Create parent directories</p></td><td><div><div><p>If selected, a parent folder is created if the target path or its parent donât exist. If unselected, a <code>FILE:ILLEGAL_PATH</code> error is thrown when you try to perform the operation.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Overwrite</p></td><td><p>If selected, overwrites the target file if a file already exists. If <strong>Overwrite</strong> isnât selected, a <code>FILE:FILE_ALREADY_EXISTS</code> error is thrown when you try to perform the operation.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Rename to</p></td><td><p>New name for the file. If a new name isnât provided, the original file name is kept.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

2.  Click the **Advanced** tab to configure the following attributes:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Attribute</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Creates custom error types that you map to Mule error types. The mappings can help you further differentiate and group errors in your app. This attribute is not currently enabled in the configuration UI. Configure it in the XML editor. See <a href="../../mule-runtime/latest/mule-error-concept">Mule Errors</a>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="3"><p>Connection</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection strategy</p></td><td><div><div><p>When an operation in a Mule application fails to connect to an external server, the default behavior is for the operation to fail immediately and return a connectivity error. You can modify the default behavior by configuring the reconnection strategy for the operation. This attribute is not currently enabled in the configuration UI. Configure it in the XML editor. See <a href="file-documentation#reconnect">Reconnect</a> and <a href="file-documentation#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a>, and <a href="#configuring-reconnection-strategy">Configuring the Reconnection Strategy in XML</a>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Configuring the Create Directory Operation

The **Create Directory** operation creates a directory in the specified **Directory path**.

1.  Click the **Create Directory** operation on the canvas UI to configure the following attributes
    in the **General** tab:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Attribute</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Directory path</p></td><td><p>New directoryâs path name, for example <code>~/newFolder</code>.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

2.  Click the **Advanced** tab to configure the following attributes:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Attribute</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Creates custom error types that you map to Mule error types. The mappings can help you further differentiate and group errors in your app. This attribute is not currently enabled in the configuration UI. Configure it in the XML editor. See <a href="../../mule-runtime/latest/mule-error-concept">Mule Errors</a>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="3"><p>Connection</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection strategy</p></td><td><div><div><p>When an operation in a Mule application fails to connect to an external server, the default behavior is for the operation to fail immediately and return a connectivity error. You can modify the default behavior by configuring the reconnection strategy for the operation. This attribute is not currently enabled in the configuration UI. Configure it in the XML editor. See <a href="file-documentation#reconnect">Reconnect</a> and <a href="file-documentation#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a>, and <a href="#configuring-reconnection-strategy">Configuring the Reconnection Strategy in XML</a>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Configuring the Delete Operation

The **Delete** operation deletes the file specified in the **Directory path**, as long as the file
isnât locked. This operation doesnât delete directories.

1.  Click the **Delete** operation on the canvas UI to configure the following attributes in the
    **General** tab:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Attribute</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Directory path</p></td><td><p>Path to the file to delete.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

2.  Click the **Advanced** tab to configure the following attributes:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Attribute</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Creates custom error types that you map to Mule error types. The mappings can help you further differentiate and group errors in your app. This attribute is not currently enabled in the configuration UI. Configure it in the XML editor. See <a href="../../mule-runtime/latest/mule-error-concept">Mule Errors</a>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="3"><p>Connection</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection strategy</p></td><td><div><div><p>When an operation in a Mule application fails to connect to an external server, the default behavior is for the operation to fail immediately and return a connectivity error. You can modify the default behavior by configuring the reconnection strategy for the operation. This attribute is not currently enabled in the configuration UI. Configure it in the XML editor. See <a href="file-documentation#reconnect">Reconnect</a> and <a href="file-documentation#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a>, and <a href="#configuring-reconnection-strategy">Configuring the Reconnection Strategy in XML</a>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Configuring the List Operation

Use the **List** operation to list all files in the specified **Directory path**.

1.  Click the **List** operation on the canvas UI to configure the following attributes in the
    **General** tab:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Attribute</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Directory path</p></td><td><p>Path to the directory with the files to list.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Recursive</p></td><td><p>If selected, the contents of sub-directories are also listed in the output.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File Matching Rules</p></td><td><div><div><p>Filters files based on the matching criteria that you use for accepting or rejecting a file. For an example of file matching rules, see <a href="file-list#file-matcher">Filter Files by Matching Criteria</a>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

2.  Click the **Advanced** tab to configure the following attributes:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Attribute</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Time between size check</p></td><td><p>Wait time, in milliseconds, between size checks to determine if a file is ready to read. This allows a file write to complete before processing. Mule runtime performs two size checks and waits the specified amount of time between calls. If both checks return the same value, the file is ready to read.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Time between size check unit</p></td><td><p>Time unit for <strong>Time between size check</strong>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Subset</p></td><td><p>Limits and sorts the number of files returned.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming strategy</p></td><td><p>Configures the way data streams are consumed in Mule apps. This attribute is not currently enabled in the configuration UI. Configure it in the XML editor. See <a href="#configuring-streaming-strategy">Configuring the Streaming Strategy in XML</a>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><p>Name of the variable on which the operationâs output is placed.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><p>Expression thatâs evaluated against the operationâs output. The outcome of this expression is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Creates custom error types that you map to Mule error types. The mappings can help you further differentiate and group errors in your app. This attribute is not currently enabled in the configuration UI. Configure it in the XML editor. See <a href="../../mule-runtime/latest/mule-error-concept">Mule Errors</a>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection strategy</p></td><td><div><div><p>When an operation in a Mule application fails to connect to an external server, the default behavior is for the operation to fail immediately and return a connectivity error. You can modify the default behavior by configuring the reconnection strategy for the operation. This attribute is not currently enabled in the configuration UI. Configure it in the XML editor. See <a href="file-documentation#reconnect">Reconnect</a> and <a href="file-documentation#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a>, and <a href="#configuring-reconnection-strategy">Configuring the Reconnection Strategy in XML</a>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Configuring the Move Operation

The **Move** operation moves the file or directory from the **Source path** to the **Target Path**.
If the **Source path** points to a directory, then the directory is moved recursively.

1.  Click the **Move** operation on the canvas UI to configure the following attributes in the
    **General** tab:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Attribute</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Source path</p></td><td><p>Path to the file or directory to perform the operation on.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target path</p></td><td><p>Path to the directory to place the file in.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Create parent directories</p></td><td><div><div><p>If selected, a parent folder is created if the target path or its parent donât exist. If unselected, a <code>FILE:ILLEGAL_PATH</code> error is thrown when you try to perform the operation.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Overwrite</p></td><td><p>If selected, overwrites the target file if a file already exists. If <strong>Overwrite</strong> isnât selected, a <code>FILE:FILE_ALREADY_EXISTS</code> error is thrown when you try to perform the operation.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Rename to</p></td><td><p>New name for the file. If a new name isnât provided, the original file name is kept.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

2.  Click the **Advanced** tab to configure the following attributes:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Attribute</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Creates custom error types that you map to Mule error types. The mappings can help you further differentiate and group errors in your app. This attribute is not currently enabled in the configuration UI. Configure it in the XML editor. See <a href="../../mule-runtime/latest/mule-error-concept">Mule Errors</a>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="3"><p>Connection</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection strategy</p></td><td><div><div><p>When an operation in a Mule application fails to connect to an external server, the default behavior is for the operation to fail immediately and return a connectivity error. You can modify the default behavior by configuring the reconnection strategy for the operation. This attribute is not currently enabled in the configuration UI. Configure it in the XML editor. See <a href="file-documentation#reconnect">Reconnect</a> and <a href="file-documentation#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a>, and <a href="#configuring-reconnection-strategy">Configuring the Reconnection Strategy in XML</a>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Configuring the Read Operation

The **Read** operation reads the contents and metadata of a file at the specified path and returns a
message whose payload is an input stream containing the fileâs contents. The metadata is represented
as a `LocalFileAttributes` object thatâs expressed as message attributes
(`Message#getAttributes()`).

The fileâs MIME type is based on the fileâs extension. You can force the output encoding and MIME
type by using the optional **Output MIME type** and **Output encoding** parameters.

1.  Click the **Read** operation on the canvas UI to configure the following attributes in the
    **General** tab:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Attribute</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>File Path</p></td><td><p>Path to the file to read.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

2.  Click the **Advanced** tab to configure the following attributes:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Attribute</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Lock</p></td><td><p>If selected, places a file system-level lock on the file until the input stream that the operation returns is closed or fully consumed. The lock is provided by the host file system, so its behavior might change depending on the mounted drive and the operation system on which Mule runtime is running.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Time between size check</p></td><td><p>Wait time, in milliseconds, between size checks to determine if a file is ready to read. This allows a file write to complete before processing. Mule runtime performs two size checks and waits the specified amount of time between calls. If both checks return the same value, the file is ready to read.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Time between size check unit</p></td><td><p>Time unit for <strong>Time between size check</strong>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output MIME type</p></td><td><p>MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Parameters</p></td><td><p>One or more key-value pairs to append to the value of the <strong>Output MIME type</strong> attribute. Multiple key-value pairs are allowed. These key-value pairs enable you to store data in a variable so that any component in the flow can use it.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output encoding</p></td><td><p>Encoding of the payload that this component outputs.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><p>Configure the way data streams are consumed in Mule apps. This attribute is not currently enabled in the configuration UI. Configure it in the XML editor. See <a href="#configuring-streaming-strategy">Configuring the Streaming Strategy in XML</a>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><p>Name of the variable on which the operationâs output is placed.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><p>Expression thatâs evaluated against the operationâs output. The outcome of this expression is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Creates custom error types that you map to Mule error types. The mappings can help you further differentiate and group errors in your app. This attribute is not currently enabled in the configuration UI. Configure it in the XML editor. See <a href="../../mule-runtime/latest/mule-error-concept">Mule Errors</a>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection strategy</p></td><td><div><div><p>When an operation in a Mule application fails to connect to an external server, the default behavior is for the operation to fail immediately and return a connectivity error. You can modify the default behavior by configuring the reconnection strategy for the operation. This attribute is not currently enabled in the configuration UI. Configure it in the XML editor. See <a href="file-documentation#reconnect">Reconnect</a> and <a href="file-documentation#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a>, and <a href="#configuring-reconnection-strategy">Configuring the Reconnection Strategy in XML</a>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Configuring the Rename Operation

The **Rename** operation renames the file specified in the **Path** field to the name provided in
the `to` parameter. If the `to` parameter contains path separators, a `FILE:ILLEGAL_PATH` is thrown.

1.  Click the **Rename** operation on the canvas UI to configure the following attributes in the
    **General** tab:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Attribute</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Path</p></td><td><p>Path to the file to rename.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>New Name</p></td><td><p>New name for the file.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Overwrite</p></td><td><p>If selected, overwrites the target file if a file already exists with the name you specified in <strong>New Name</strong>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

2.  Click the **Advanced** tab to configure the following attributes:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Attribute</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Creates custom error types that you map to Mule error types. The mappings can help you further differentiate and group errors in your app. This attribute is not currently enabled in the configuration UI. Configure it in the XML editor. See <a href="../../mule-runtime/latest/mule-error-concept">Mule Errors</a>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="3"><p>Connection</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection strategy</p></td><td><div><div><p>When an operation in a Mule application fails to connect to an external server, the default behavior is for the operation to fail immediately and return a connectivity error. You can modify the default behavior by configuring the reconnection strategy for the operation. This attribute is not currently enabled in the configuration UI. Configure it in the XML editor. See <a href="file-documentation#reconnect">Reconnect</a> and <a href="file-documentation#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a>, and <a href="#configuring-reconnection-strategy">Configuring the Reconnection Strategy in XML</a>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Configuring the Write Operation

The **Write** operation writes content into the file specified in **Path**.

1.  Click the **Write** operation on the canvas UI to configure the following attributes in the
    **General** tab:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Attribute</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Path</p></td><td><p>Path to the directory that contains the file to write content to.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><p>Content to write to the file. Defaults to the <strong>Content</strong> payload.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Create parent directories</p></td><td><div><div><p>If selected, a parent folder is created if the target path or its parent donât exist. If unselected, a <code>FILE:ILLEGAL_PATH</code> error is thrown when you try to perform the operation.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

2.  Click the **Advanced** tab to configure the following attributes:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Attribute</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Creates custom error types that you map to Mule error types. The mappings can help you further differentiate and group errors in your app. This attribute is not currently enabled in the configuration UI. Configure it in the XML editor. See <a href="../../mule-runtime/latest/mule-error-concept">Mule Errors</a>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="3"><p>Connection</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection strategy</p></td><td><div><div><p>When an operation in a Mule application fails to connect to an external server, the default behavior is for the operation to fail immediately and return a connectivity error. You can modify the default behavior by configuring the reconnection strategy for the operation. This attribute is not currently enabled in the configuration UI. Configure it in the XML editor. See <a href="file-documentation#reconnect">Reconnect</a> and <a href="file-documentation#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a>, and <a href="#configuring-reconnection-strategy">Configuring the Reconnection Strategy in XML</a>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

3.  Select **Lock** to place a file-system-level lock on the file until the input stream that the
    operation returns is closed or fully consumed.

## Configuring the Scheduling Strategy in XML

### Fixed Frequency

This is an example of the fixed frequency scheduling strategy XML using the default values:

```xml
<flow name="db-connector-acbFlow" doc:id="1c863ef7-d9e6-48ab-be8e-f1465d2b619d" >
	<db:listener doc:name="On Table Row" doc:id="81d4cf34-bb0d-497c-bc3f-316f4e79ad85" >
		<scheduling-strategy >
			<fixed-frequency frequency="1000" startDelay="0" timeUnit="MILLISECONDS"/>
		</scheduling-strategy>
	</db:listener>
</flow>
```

### Cron

For more complex scheduling strategies, you can use a cron expression:

```xml
<flow name="db-connector-acbFlow" doc:id="1c863ef7-d9e6-48ab-be8e-f1465d2b619d" >
	<db:listener doc:name="On Table Row" doc:id="81d4cf34-bb0d-497c-bc3f-316f4e79ad85" >
    <scheduling-strategy>
      <cron expression="0 0 12 * * ?" timeZone="America/Los_Angeles"/>
    </scheduling-strategy>
  </db:listener>
</flow>
```

## Configuring the Reconnection Strategy in XML

The following is an XML example of a standard reconnection strategy setting the count of
reconnection attempts before failing to `5` and setting `failsDeployment` to true, which causes the
application deployment to fail if the connection test fails. Because a frequency is not specified,
the retry interval is the default of every 2000 ms:

```xml
<db:config name="Database_Config" doc:name="Database Config" doc:id="79ae8d0f-e0af-4901-961d-b80befbf3f86" >
	<db:data-source-connection >
		<reconnection failsDeployment="true" >
           <reconnect count="5"/>
        </reconnection>
	</db:data-source-connection>
</db:config>
```

The following is an XML example of a reconnect forever strategy where the connector attempts to
reconnect every 4000 ms for as long as the application runs:

```xml
<db:config name="Database_Config1" doc:name="Database Config" doc:id="c0aaf1dd-ab6e-49ee-9e4d-8b5799945cc9">
    <db:data-source-connection>
        <reconnection failsDeployment="true">
            <reconnection>
                <reconnect-forever frequency="4000"/>
            </reconnection>
        </reconnection>
    </db:data-source-connection>
</db:config>
```

## Configuring the Streaming Strategy in XML

You can configure how Mule handles data streams with streaming strategies.

### File-Stored Repeatable Stream

The following is an XML example of reading a file that is configured with a 1 MB buffer:

```xml
<file:read path="bigFile.json">
  <repeatable-file-store-stream
    inMemorySize="1"
    bufferUnit="MB"/>
</file:read>
```

If you consistently process a file thatâs no larger than 10 KB, you can save memory by reducing the
`inMemorySize` attribute:

```xml
<file:read path="smallFile.json">
  <repeatable-file-store-stream
    inMemorySize="10"
    bufferUnit="KB"/>
 </file:read>
```

### In-Memory Repeatable Stream

The following XML example shows an in-memory repeatable stream with a 512 KB initial size, which
grows at a rate of 256 KB and allows up to 2000 KB (2 MB) of content in memory:

```xml
<file:read path="exampleFile.json">
  <repeatable-in-memory-stream
    initialBufferSize="512"
    bufferSizeIncrement="256"
    maxInMemorySize="2000"
    bufferUnit="KB"/>
</file:read>
```
---

# Copy and Move Files

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for File (File Connector) provides the **Copy** and **Move** operations that
enable you to copy or move files or directories on demand.

- **Source path**  
  The directory path name or file name to copy or move. If a file or directory already exists and
  the **Overwrite** field is set to `false`, the connector throws the message
  `FILE:FILE_ALREADY_EXISTS`.
- **Target path**  
  The directory path name into which to copy or move the file. If a target path doesnât exist, and
  neither does its parent, the connector attempts to create the path depending on the value of the
  **Create parent directories** argument. If **Create parent directories** is `false`, then
  `FILE:ILLEGAL_PATH` occurs.
- **Rename to**  
  Renames the file as part of the operation. Set this field with a file name instead of a path. The
  connector retains the original file name if you do not configure this field.

## Configure the Copy Operation in Studio

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, search for `file` and select the **Copy** operation.
2.  Drag the **Copy** operation onto the Studio canvas.
3.  In the **General** tab of the operation configuration screen, click the plus sign (**+**) next
    to the **Connector configuration** field to access the global element configuration fields.
4.  Specify the connection information and click **OK**.
5.  Set **Source path** to the path of the file to copy, for example,
    `Documents/source-folder/file.txt`.
6.  Set **Target path** to the directory path into which to copy the file, for example,
    `Documents/target-folder`.
7.  Set **Rename to** to rename the file as part of the operation, for example, `renamed.txt`.  
    This field must be a filename, not a path. If this attribute is not provided, the original
    filename is used.

![Copy operation configuration in Studio](_images/file-copy-operation.png)

In the **Configuration XML** editor, the `<file:copy>` configuration looks like this:

```xml
<flow name="file-connector-testFlow" >
		<http:listener config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/copy"/>
		<file:copy doc:name="Copy" sourcePath="Documents/source-folder/file.txt" targetPath="Documents/target-folder" config-ref="File_Config" overwrite="true" renameTo="renamed.txt"/>
	</flow>
```

## Configure the Move Operation in Studio

To add and configure the **Move** operation in Studio, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, search for `ftp` and select the **Move** operation.
2.  Drag the **Move** operation onto the Studio canvas.
3.  In the **General** tab of the operation configuration screen, click the plus sign (**+**) next
    to the **Connector configuration** field to access the global element configuration fields.
4.  Specify the connection information and click **OK**.
5.  Set **Source path** to the path of the file to move, for example, `source.txt`.
6.  Set **Target path** to the directory path into which to move the file, for example, `backup`.
7.  Set **Rename to** to rename the file as part of the operation, for example, `renamed.txt`.  
    This field must be a filename, not a path. If this attribute is not provided, the original
    filename is used.

![Move operation configuration in Studio](_images/file-move-operation.png)

In the **Configuration XML** editor, the `<file:move>` configuration looks like this:

```xml
<flow name="file-connector-testFlow" >
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/copy"/>
		<file:move doc:name="Move" config-ref="File_Config" sourcePath="source.txt" targetPath="backup" renameTo="renamed.txt"/>
	</flow>
```

## See Also

- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Create a Directory Using File Connector Example

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for File (File Connector) provides a **Create directory** operation that enables
you to create a directory of a given name.

If the reason for creating the directory is to immediately write, copy, or move content to the
directory, then use the **Write**, **Copy**, or **Move** operations with the **Create parent
directories** field set to **TRUE**.

## Configure the Create Directory Operation in Studio

To add and configure the **Create directory** operation in Studio, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, search for `file` and select the **Create directory** operation.
2.  Drag the **Create directory** operation onto the Studio canvas.
3.  In the **General** tab of the operation configuration screen, click the plus sign (**+**) next
    to the **Connector configuration** field to access the global element configuration fields.
4.  Specify the connection information and click **OK**.
5.  Set **Directory path** to the new directoryâs path name, for example, `~/newFolder`.

![Create directory operation configuration in Studio](_images/file-create-directory-operation.png)

In the **Configuration XML** editor, the `<file:create-directory>` configuration looks like this:

```xml
<file:create-directory doc:name="Create directory"  config-ref="File_Config" directoryPath="~/newFolder"/>
```

## See Also

- [File Connector Examples](file-examples)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# File Connector Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

File Connector v1.5

Anypoint Connector for File (File Connector) manipulates file systems mounted on a host operation
system.

This class serves as both extension definition and configuration. Operations are based on the
standard `BaseFileSystemOperations`.

Release Notes: [File Connector Release Notes](../../release-notes/connector/connector-file)

## Configurations

---

### Default Configuration

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#config_connection">Local FileSystem Connection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>The connection types that can be provided to this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Default write encoding (DEPRECATED)</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter is deprecated and is ignored.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p><a href="#time-between-size-check">Time Between Size Check</a></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Wait time between size checks to determine if a file is ready to read. This allows a file write to complete before processing. If no value is provided, the check is not performed. When enabled, Mule performs two size checks, waiting the specified time between calls. If both checks return the same value, the file is ready to be read. This attribute works in tandem with <strong>Time between size check unit</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Time Between Size Check Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A <strong>Time unit</strong> that qualifies the <strong>Time between size check</strong> attribute.</p></td><td><p><code>MILLISECONDS</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expiration Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before the runtime considers it eligible for expiration. This does not mean that the platform will expire the instance at the exact moment that it becomes eligible. The runtime will actually purge the instances when it sees it fit.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Connection Types

##### Local FileSystem Connection

A Connection Provider that provides instances of File System from instances of File Connector.

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Working Directory</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The directory to consider the root of every relative path used with this connector. If not provided, it defaults to the value of the <code>user.home</code> system property. If that system property is not set, then the connector fails to initialize.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When an application deploys, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Operations

- [Copy](#copy)
- [Create Directory](#createDirectory)
- [Delete](#delete)
- [List](#list)
- [Move](#move)
- [Read](#read)
- [Rename](#rename)
- [Write](#write)

## Associated Sources

- [On New or Updated File](#listener)

### Copy

`<file:copy>`

Copies the file or directory specified in **Source Path** into the **Target Path**. The source path
can be either a file or a directory. If it points to a directory, then it is copied recursively.

If the target path doesnât exist, and neither does its parent, then a parent folder is created if
**Create parent directories** is set to `true`. If **Create parent directories** is set to `false`,
then a `FILE:ILLEGAL_PATH` error is thrown.

If **Overwrite** is set to `true` and the target file already exists, then the target file is
overwritten. Otherwise, a `FILE:FILE_ALREADY_EXISTS` error is thrown.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Source Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The path to the file to be copied.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The target directory where to copy the file.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Create Parent Directories</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether or not to create parent directories if they donât exist.</p></td><td><p><code>true</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Overwrite</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether or not to overwrite the file if the target destination already exists.</p></td><td><p><code>false</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Rename To</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The new name for the copied file. If not provided, original file name is kept.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- FILE:CONNECTIVITY
- FILE:ILLEGAL_PATH
- FILE:FILE_ALREADY_EXISTS
- FILE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

### Create Directory

`<file:create-directory>`

Creates a new directory on **Directory path**.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Directory Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The new directoryâs name.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- FILE:CONNECTIVITY
- FILE:ILLEGAL_PATH
- FILE:FILE_ALREADY_EXISTS
- FILE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- FILE:ACCESS_DENIED

### Delete

`<file:delete>`

Deletes the file that the path field points to, provided that the file is not locked.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The path to the file to be deleted.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- FILE:CONNECTIVITY
- FILE:ILLEGAL_PATH
- FILE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- FILE:ACCESS_DENIED

### List

`<file:list>`

Lists all the files in the **Directory path** that match a matcher.

If the listing encounters a directory, the output list includes its contents depending on the value
of the **Recursive** parameter.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Directory Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The path to the directory to be listed.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Recursive</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to include the contents of subdirectories.</p></td><td><p><code>false</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>File Matching Rules</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#matcher">Matcher</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>A matcher used to filter the output list.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p><a href="#time-between-size-check">Time Between Size Check</a></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Wait time between size checks to determine if a file is ready to read.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Time Between Size Check Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit to be used in the wait time between size checks.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Subset</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#LocalSubsetList">Local Subset List</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Limit and sort the number of files returned.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-iterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-iterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of a variable on which the operationâs output is placed.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and the outcome of that expression is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Message of [CursorProvider] payload and [<a href="#LocalFileAttributes">Local File Attributes</a>] attributes</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- FILE:ILLEGAL_PATH
- FILE:ACCESS_DENIED

### Move

`<file:move>`

Moves the file or directory from the **Source Path** into the **Target Path**. The source path can
be either a file or a directory. If it points to a directory, then it will be moved recursively.

If the target path doesnât exist, and neither does its parent, then a parent folder is created if
**Create parent directories** is set to `true`. If **Create parent directories** is set to `false`,
then a `FILE:ILLEGAL_PATH` error is thrown.

If the target file already exists, then it will be overwritten if **Overwrite** is set to `true`. If
**Overwrite** is set to `false`, a `FILE:FILE_ALREADY_EXISTS` error will be thrown.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Source Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The path to the file to be copied.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The target directory.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Create Parent Directories</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether or not to attempt creating any parent directories which donât exists.</p></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Overwrite</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether or not overwrite the file if the target destination already exists.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Rename To</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Moved fileâs new name. If not provided, original file name is kept.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- FILE:CONNECTIVITY
- FILE:ILLEGAL_PATH
- FILE:FILE_ALREADY_EXISTS
- FILE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

### Read

`<file:read>`

Obtains the content and metadata of a file at a given path. The operation itself returns a Message
which payload is a InputStream with the fileâs content, and the metadata is represent as a
LocalFileAttributes object thatâs placed as the message `Message#getAttributes()` attributes.

If the lock parameter is set to true, then a file system level lock is placed on the file until the
input stream this operation returns is closed or fully consumed. Because the lock is actually
provided by the host file system, its behavior might change depending on the mounted drive and the
operation system on which Mule is running. Take that into consideration before blindly relying on
this lock.

This method also makes a best effort to determine the MIME type of the file being read. The fileâs
extension is used to make an educated guess on the fileâs MIME type. The user also has the chance to
force the output encoding and `mimeType` through the `outputEncoding` and `outputMimeType` optional
parameters.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The path to the file to be read</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Lock</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether or not to lock the file. Defaults to false.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p><a href="#time-between-size-check">Time Between Size Check</a></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Wait time between size checks to determine if a file is ready to be read.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Time Between Size Check Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>time unit to be used in the wait time between size checks.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-stream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-stream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behavior</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of a variable on which the operationâs output will be placed</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and the outcome of that expression will be stored in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#LocalFileAttributes">Local File Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- FILE:CONNECTIVITY
- FILE:FILE_LOCK
- FILE:ILLEGAL_PATH
- FILE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- FILE:ACCESS_DENIED

### Rename

`<file:rename>`

Renames the file pointed by the path to the name provided on the `to` parameter. The `to` argument
should not contain any path separator. FILE:ILLEGAL_PATH is thrown if this precondition is not
honored.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Configuration</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p><strong>x</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Path</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The path to the file to be renamed.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p><strong>x</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>New Name</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The fileâs new name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p><strong>x</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Overwrite</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether or not overwrite the file if the target destination already exists.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- FILE:CONNECTIVITY
- FILE:ILLEGAL_PATH
- FILE:FILE_ALREADY_EXISTS
- FILE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- FILE:ACCESS_DENIED

### Write

`<file:write>`

Writes the content into the file pointed by path.

If the directory on which the file is attempting to be written doesnât exist, then the operation
will either throw FILE:ILLEGAL_PATH error or create such folder depending on the value of the
createParentDirectory.

If the file itself already exists, then the behavior depends on the supplied mode.

This operation also supports locking support depending on the value of the lock argument, but
following the same rules and considerations as described in the read operation.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The path of the file to be written</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><p>The content to be written into the file. Defaults to the current Message payload</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Encoding (DEPRECATED)</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter is deprecated and does nothing if configured.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Create Parent Directories</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether or not to attempt creating any parent directories which donât exists.</p></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Lock</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether or not to lock the file. Defaults to false</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Write Mode</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>OVERWRITE</p></li><li><p>APPEND</p></li><li><p>CREATE_NEW</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>a FileWriteMode. Defaults to OVERWRITE</p></td><td><p>OVERWRITE</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- FILE:ILLEGAL_CONTENT
- FILE:CONNECTIVITY
- FILE:ILLEGAL_PATH
- FILE:FILE_ALREADY_EXISTS
- FILE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- FILE:ACCESS_DENIED

## Sources

### On New or Updated File

`<file:listener>`

Polls a directory looking for files that have been created or updated. One message is generated for
each file that is found.

The key part of this functionality is how to determine that a file is actually new.

These are the strategies for that:

- Set the `autoDelete` parameter to `true`: This deletes each processed file after it has been
  processed, causing all files obtained in the next poll to be necessarily new.
- Set `moveToDirectory` parameter: This moves each processed file to a different directory after it
  has been processed, achieving the same effect as `autoDelete<` but without losing the file.
- Use the watermarkMode parameter to pick only files that are created or updated after the last poll
  was executed.

A matcher can also be used for additional filtering of files.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Directory</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The directory on which polled files are contained.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Recursive</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether or not to also files contained in sub directories.</p></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Matcher</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#matcher">Matcher</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>A matcher used to filter events on files which do not meet the matcherâs criteria.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Watermark Mode</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>DISABLED</p></li><li><p>MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP</p></li><li><p>CREATED_TIMESTAMP</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Controls whether or not to do watermarking, and if so, if the watermark should consider the fileâs modification or creation timestamps</p></td><td><p>DISABLED</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p><a href="#time-between-size-check">Time Between Size Check</a></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Wait time in milliseconds between size checks to determine if a file is ready to be read. This allows a file write to complete before processing. You can disable this feature by omitting a value. When enabled, Mule performs two size checks waiting the specified time between calls. If both checks return the same value, the file is ready to be read.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Time Between Size Check Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A <strong>Time unit</strong> that qualifies the <strong>Time between size check</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Primary Node Only</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether this source should be executed only on the primary node when running in a cluster.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Scheduling Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><p>scheduling-strategy</p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the scheduler that triggers the polling.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-stream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-stream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Redelivery Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Auto Delete</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether each file should be deleted after processing or not.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Move To Directory</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>If provided, each processed file will be moved to a directory pointed by this path.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Rename To</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter works in tandem with <strong>Move to directory</strong>. Use this parameter to enter the name under which the file should be moved. Do not set this parameter if moveToDirectory hasnât been set as well.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Apply Post Action When Failed</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether any of the post actions (<strong>Auto delete</strong> and <strong>Move to directory</strong>) should also be applied in case the file failed to be processed. If set to <code>false</code>, no failed files are moved nor deleted.</p></td><td><p><code>true</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Overwrite</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Enables you to overwrite the target file when the destination file has the same name.</p></td><td><p><code>false</code></p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#LocalFileAttributes">Local File Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

## Types

### Reconnection

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Fails Deployment</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment will fail if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The reconnection strategy to use</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Frequency</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How often in milliseconds to reconnect.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Count</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How many reconnection attempts to make.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>blocking</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td><td><p>If false, the reconnection strategy runs in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect Forever

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Frequency</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How often in milliseconds to reconnect.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>blocking</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td><td><p>If false, the reconnection strategy runs in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Expiration Policy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Max Idle Time</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>A scalar time value for the maximum amount of time a dynamic configuration instance should be allowed to be idle before itâs considered eligible for expiration</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the <strong>Max idle time</strong> attribute.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Local File Attributes

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Last Modified Time</p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Last Access Time</p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Creation Time</p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Regular File</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Directory</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Symbolic Link</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Matcher

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Created Since</p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><p>Files created before this date are rejected. Any timezone specification in this value is ignored and the Mule serverâs time zone is used instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Created Until</p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><p>Files created after this date are rejected. Any timezone specification in this value is ignored and the Mule serverâs time zone is used instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Updated Since</p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><p>Files modified before this date are rejected. Any timezone specification in this value is ignored and the Mule serverâs time zone is used instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Updated Until</p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><p>Files modified after this date are rejected. Any timezone specification in this value is ignored and the Mule serverâs time zone is used instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Accessed Since</p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><p>Files that were last accessed before this date are rejected. Any timezone specification in this value is ignored and the Mule serverâs time zone is used instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Accessed Until</p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><p>Files that were last accessed after this date are rejected. Any timezone specification in this value is ignored and the Mule serverâs time zone is used instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Not Updated In The Last</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Minimum time that should pass since a file was last updated for it not to be rejected. This attribute works in tandem with <strong>Time unit</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Updated In The Last</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum time that should pass since a file was last updated for it not to be rejected.
